skill.name: error-handling
skill.description: Guides developers to add reliable error handling for async code, API calls, React components, logging, fallback states, and user-facing messages.
skill.summary: Improve application reliability with structured error handling patterns for frontend and API code.
skill.author: Doyajin174

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution, network, environment, weak cryptography, and dangerous combination indicators. Review found the command hits are markdown code fences, the URLs are documentation references, and the environment access appears only inside an instructional TypeScript logging example. No prompt injection or malicious exfiltration intent was found, but the skill metadata grants broad Bash and file editing tools.

low_findings.title: Broad Tool Permissions in Skill Metadata
low_findings.description: The skill declares Bash, Edit, and Write access in allowed tools. This is legitimate for a coding assistant that may inspect and modify project files, but it increases impact if future instructions are changed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The metadata explicitly grants Bash and file editing tools. The surrounding skill content does not instruct unsafe command execution, so the risk is limited to broad capability exposure.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Matches Are Markdown Fences
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are TypeScript, TSX, or markdown code fence delimiters in documentation examples. I did not find executable shell or Ruby backtick commands at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each listed location is inside markdown documentation or a fenced code block marker. There is no runtime script file or shell command execution at these lines.

low_findings.title: Documentation-Only URL and Environment References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are reference links to React and MDN documentation. The process.env.NODE_ENV access appears in a logger example and does not read secrets or transmit environment values.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation references, and the only environment access is NODE_ENV in a non-executable example. No credential variable or network exfiltration code was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Dangerous Combination Heuristics Not Supported
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography match at the description line has no cryptographic API or algorithm evidence. The dangerous combination heuristic is not supported because the underlying command, network, and credential findings are documentation-only false positives.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is descriptive metadata with no crypto implementation. The cited command, network, and environment signals do not form an executable exfiltration path.

content.value_statement: Many applications hide failures or expose technical messages to users. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to add structured handling, recovery, logging, and friendly feedback.
actual_capabilities: Identifies empty catch blocks and weak error handling patterns.
Suggests typed error branches for network, authentication, validation, and not found errors.
Provides React Error Boundary patterns for full-page and section-level failures.
Recommends Promise handling with catch blocks and Promise.allSettled.
Guides user-friendly error messages instead of raw stack traces.
Adds logging and fallback behavior for graceful degradation.
limitations: It provides patterns and examples, not automated runtime monitoring.
It does not verify third-party error tracking service configuration.
It may need project-specific error classes and message catalogs.
It does not replace security review for sensitive error logging.

use_cases.title: Harden API Calls
use_cases.description: Add typed failure handling, retry decisions, fallback data, and safe user messages around service calls.

use_cases.title: Review React Failure States
use_cases.description: Check components for error boundaries, loading states, retry options, and fallback UI.

use_cases.title: Standardize Error Messaging
use_cases.description: Create consistent user-facing messages while preserving useful logs for developers.

prompt_templates.title: Find Missing Error Handling
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this feature for missing error handling. Point out empty catch blocks, unhandled promises, and user messages that need improvement.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an API Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Add resilience to a request flow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Improve this API call flow with typed error handling, safe logging, retry guidance, fallback behavior, and user-friendly messages.

prompt_templates.title: Add React Error Boundaries
prompt_templates.scenario: Protect a React route or component tree.
prompt_templates.prompt: Add suitable React Error Boundary coverage for this component structure. Include fallback UI, error reporting, and recovery actions.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Project Error Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a project-wide error handling strategy for this codebase. Include error classes, logging, message mapping, boundaries, async handling, and graceful degradation.

output_examples.input: A data loading component fails silently when a request rejects.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the unhandled rejection, recommends a catch path, sets an error state, logs context, and shows a retry message.

output_examples.input: A dashboard depends on multiple independent API calls.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends handling each result independently, showing partial data when possible, and logging only the failed sections.

output_examples.input: A React route can crash when a chart library throws.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends wrapping the chart section in an error boundary with a focused fallback and a reload or retry action.

best_practices: Map technical errors to stable user-facing messages.
Log enough context for debugging without exposing secrets.
Use graceful fallback behavior when partial success is acceptable.
anti_patterns: Do not leave catch blocks empty.
Do not show raw stack traces or internal exception text to users.
Do not fail an entire page when one independent section can fall back.

faq.question: Does this skill execute error monitoring code?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples for patterns such as logging, boundaries, and fallback states.

faq.question: Can it help with React applications?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Error Boundary patterns, section-level fallbacks, and user recovery actions.

faq.question: Can it improve API error handling?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide handling for network errors, authentication failures, validation errors, and missing resources.

faq.question: Will it prevent all production failures?
faq.answer: No. It improves handling and recovery, but testing and monitoring are still required.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive applications?
faq.answer: Use care when logging errors. Review logs to ensure tokens, user data, and stack traces are not exposed.
